metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide
metadata.tags: Column, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Digitalisering, AI
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: ChatGPT can handle the most diverse requests. Meanwhile, companies struggle to put AI into practice. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Sometimes it is a standalone service via an Application Programming Interface (API). Other times even the full source code of the model is made public. Such a truly open-source approach is still the exception, [critics](https://techcrunch.com/2022/05/19/when-big-ai-labs-refuse-to-open-source-their-models-the-community-steps-in/?guccounter=1) in academia note. AI-as-a-Service. But while the average scientist benefits from full insight into a model, most entrepreneurs are simply looking for a way to apply AI in an accessible way. AI-as-a-Service, of which ChatGPT is an example, can help. It is AI that is offered, to a greater or lesser extent, ready-made. Many of these services offer a very specific component from the AI toolkit, for example image recognition, translation, or sentiment analysis. Virtual assistants — programmes that can answer customer questions in a chat — are also available as a service. The provider of such assistants supplies the generic technology, after which it can be tailored to the client’s specific case. In addition, there are the so-called machine learning frameworks. These provide the building blocks to create AI models yourself relatively easily, including algorithms that can still be trained on client-owned data. The fact that smaller companies have not yet started experimenting on a large scale is because AI is usually not just a matter of plugging in. First, technological expertise is essential to identify the possible application areas of AI in a company, and then actually integrate it into operations. In a time when IT staff are scarce, that creates challenges. In addition, many AI solutions must first be fed with data from the specific company. Those data are by no means always available.

metadata.description: ChatGPT can handle the most diverse requests. Meanwhile, companies struggle to put AI into practice. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Large companies use more AI. Logically, smaller companies in particular suffer from a lack of IT expertise and data. This is also clear when looking more closely at the CBS figures; there is a clear link between company size and AI use. Of the largest companies, as many as 48 per cent applied AI in 2020 — a big difference from the 8 per cent of the smallest companies. However, these figures do not reflect that AI is quietly making its way into all kinds of everyday applications.

Newer cars are also packed with AI features, such as automatic parking aids and braking systems. To quote the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom: “Once something is sufficiently useful and normal, it is no longer called AI.”

metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide
metadata.tags: Column, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Digitalisering, AI
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: ChatGPT can handle the most diverse requests. Meanwhile, companies struggle to put AI into practice. Read more.
metadata.parent_text:
Newer cars are also packed with AI features, such as automatic parking aids and braking systems. To quote the Swedish philosopher Nick Bostrom: “Once something is sufficiently useful and normal, it is no longer called AI.”
Persistent AI divide. OpenAI’s chatbot is of a very different order, and as such symbolises the AI development divide between the tech giants and the rest of the world. Yet the ubiquity of less spectacular AI — which we therefore do not recognise as such — teaches us that the futuristic technologies of today eventually find their way to the masses. The fact that large-scale experimentation with ChatGPT is now taking place and that people are reflecting on how such technology will change [education](https://www.nu.nl/algemeen/6247374/nieuwe-uitdaging-op-scholen-scholieren-laten-huiswerk-door-chatgpt-maken.html) also points in that direction.

text: As soon as one AI technology becomes embedded and is therefore no longer seen as AI, the next spectacular AI model rolls off big tech’s production line. If the big tech companies choose to do this open source and thereby share more in-depth knowledge with academia and business, this would at least make the AI divide a little less deep.
